identity.canonical_title: Constrained Dipole Configurations and Field Solution: U_mag

theory.canonical_statement: The magnetic energy density is \[ u_{\mathrm{mag}}(\mathbf{r},t) = \frac{1}{2\mu_0}\,\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)\cdot\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)\, \] so that the total magnetic energy inside radius $R$ is \[ U_{\mathrm{mag}} = \int_0^R\int_0^\pi\int_0^{2\pi} \frac{|\mathbf{B}(\mathbf{r},t)|^2}{2\mu_0}\,r^2\sin\theta\,dr\,d\theta\,d\phi\,. \] Mechanical compression of the dipoles also contributes work. If an external force $F_{\mathrm{mech}}$ acts to maintain separation $d$, the \emph{constraint energy} functional can be written \[ U_{\mathrm{constraint}} = U_{\mathrm{mag}} + F_{\mathrm{mech}}\,d\, \] with $F_{\mathrm{mech}} = -\nabla_d U_{\mathrm{interaction}}$. In the point-dipole limit, the force between opposite dipoles is approximately \[ F_{\mathrm{mech}} = -\nabla_d \biggl[-\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{(\boldsymbol{\mu}_1\cdot\bo
theory.technical_interpretation: Constrained Dipole Configurations and Field Solution: U_mag is an integral expression, written U_mag = ∫_0^R∫_0^π∫_0^2π (|B(r,t)|^2)/(2μ_0) r^2sinθ dr dθ dφ .. It is obtained by integrating over a domain, accumulating contributions across the region of interest. It is built from π, θ, φ, R, B, r, defining U_mag. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
